message.content: Adversaries may exploit Microsoft Outlook forms to maintain persistence on compromised systems. Outlook forms serve as templates for email presentation and functionality. By creating custom forms that execute code upon receiving a specially crafted email, attackers can ensure their malicious forms are loaded each time Outlook starts. Tools like Ruler can automate this process, making it easier for adversaries to deploy and maintain their foothold. The impact includes unauthorized code execution and potential data exfiltration whenever Outlook is used. Common exploitation methods involve sending crafted emails that trigger the malicious forms. Mitigation strategies include disabling the use of custom forms in Outlook, monitoring for unusual form activity, and employing email filtering to detect and block suspicious emails. Regularly updating and patching Outlook can also help prevent exploitation.
